title: NFS volumes created using podman volume create doesn't work
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

When I try to create a NFS volume with podman, it is created but no connection is performed against the the NFS server and the created volume is empty.
Very similar bug #4303 was reported and fixed in https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/32266d155fe156ef562d8fed27112d5644fd8c77 compare to the tests shown in that issue, I kept `tcpdump` opened and no traffic is generated during the creation of the volume and while accessing the created volume.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create a volume backed by an NFS filesystem
```
[root@testhost ~]# podman volume create --opt type=nfs --opt device=:/work --opt o=vers=4.2,rw,noatime,proto=tcp,sec=sys,local_lock=none,addr=10.160.0.10 testnfs
testnfs
```
2.
```
[root@testhost ~]# podman volume ls
DRIVER      VOLUME NAME
local       testnfs
[root@testhost ~]# podman volume inspect testnfs
[
    {
        "Name": "testnfs",
        "Driver": "local",
        "Mountpoint": "/var/lib/containers/storage/volumes/testnfs/_data",
        "CreatedAt": "2021-03-02T19:07:51.049053316+01:00",
        "Labels": {},
        "Scope": "local",
        "Options": {
            "device": ":/work",
            "o": "vers=4.2,rw,noatime,proto=tcp,sec=sys,local_lock=none,addr=10.160.0.10",
            "type": "nfs"
        }
    }
]
```
with debug
```
[root@testhost ~]# podman volume rm testnfs
testnfs
[root@testhost ~]# podman --log-level=debug volume create --opt type=nfs --opt device=:/work --opt o=vers=4.2,rw,noatime,proto=tcp,sec=sys,local_lock=none,addr=10.160.0.10 testnfs
```

```
[root@testhost ~]# ls -la /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes/testnfs/_data/
total 8
drwxr-xr-x 2 root root 4096 Mar  2 19:11 .
drwx------ 3 root root 4096 Mar  2 19:11 ..
```

3.

**Describe the results you received:**
 The NFS share is not mounted.

**Describe the results you expected:**
 The NFS share is mounted.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Exports are visible to host:
```
showmount -e 10.160.0.10
Export list for 10.160.0.10:
/work  10.100.0.0/255.255.0.0
```
Exports can be mounted outside of podman using `sudo mount -t nfs -o vers=4.2,rw,noatime,proto=tcp,sec=sys,local_lock=none 10.160.0.10:/work /mnt ` so it does not appear to be an issue with the NFS server.

I even tried with less argouments like `--opt o=vers=rw,addr=10.160.0.10` but the result is the same. Anyway the most interesting part for me is that **tcpdump doesn't show any traffic**, while when I use `mount` directly works and traffic is shown.

comments.body:
Please try:
`podman volume create --opt type=nfs --opt device=10.160.0.10:/work --opt o=vers=4.2,rw,noatime,proto=tcp,sec=sys,local_lock=none testnfs`

> Please try:
> `podman volume create --opt type=nfs --opt device=10.160.0.10:/work --opt o=vers=4.2,rw,noatime,proto=tcp,sec=sys,local_lock=none testnfs`

Same old story

Fundamentally, `podman volume create` on NFS volumes is just calling `mount` with the following format:

`mount -t <type> -o <opt> <device> <podman-managed mountpoint>`

Where `type` is from `--opt type=`, `opt` is from `--opt o=`, and `device` is from `--opt device=`. If you can get a working `mount` invocation to mount your NFS share, and then decompose it according to this rule, `podman volume create` will also work.

```
[root@testhost ~]# ll /mnt | wc -l
1
[root@testhost ~]# mount -t nfs -o vers=4.2,rw,noatime,proto=tcp,sec=sys,local_lock=none 10.160.0.10:/work /mnt
[root@testhost ~]# ll /mnt | wc -l
20
[root@testhost ~]# umount /mnt
[root@testhost ~]# ll /mnt | wc -l
1
[root@testhost ~]# podman volume create --opt type=nfs --opt device=10.160.0.10:/work --opt o=vers=4.2,rw,noatime,proto=tcp,sec=sys,local_lock=none testnfs
testnfs
[root@testhost ~]# podman volume inspect testnfs | grep Mountpoint
        "Mountpoint": "/var/lib/containers/storage/volumes/testnfs/_data",
[root@testhost ~]# ls -la /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes/testnfs/_data
total 8
drwxr-xr-x 2 root root 4096 Mar  2 19:47 .
drwx------ 3 root root 4096 Mar  2 19:47 ..
```
It doesn't seems to work for me
The volume will not be mounted unless a container is using it - so unless there is a mounting container the directory is just an empty mountpoint.
Also note that the container must be running - the volume is mounted when the first container using it starts, and unmounted when the last container using it stops.
Followed your suggestion and it works exactly as you described. Thank you very much!
